---
title: "06_analysis"
author: "Vinci Chan"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc_expand: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# 1. Setup

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(arrow)

source("R/config.R")
source("R/utils_dates.R")
```

# 2. Data Import

```{r}
holdext_clean <- open_dataset(file.path(cfg$derived_dir, "holdext_clean_parquet"))
```

# 3. Functions

## 3.1. Manager Selection

```{r}
calculate_fund_returns <- function(holdings_data) {
  holdings_data %>%
    to_duckdb() %>%
    group_by(cik, rquarter) %>%
    summarise(
      total_value = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_holdings = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    collect() %>%
    arrange(cik, rquarter) %>%
    group_by(cik) %>%
    mutate(
      prev_value = lag(total_value),
      quarterly_return = (total_value - prev_value) / prev_value
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
}

get_top_performers <- function(returns_data, horizon_quarters, latest_qtr, n_top) {
  returns_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(quarterly_return)) %>%
    mutate(quarters_ago = quarter_diff(rquarter, latest_qtr)) %>%
    filter(quarters_ago <= horizon_quarters) %>%
    group_by(cik) %>%
    filter(n() == horizon_quarters) %>%
    summarise(
      n_period = n(),
      mean_return = mean(quarterly_return),
      sd_return = sd(quarterly_return),
      sharpe_ratio = if_else(sd_return == 0, NA_real_, mean_return / sd_return),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(sharpe_ratio)) %>%
    slice_head(n = n_top)
}
```

## 3.2. Portfolio Similarity

```{r}
get_holdings_at_period_start <- function(holdings_data, top_performers_tbl) {
  holdings_data %>%
    to_duckdb() %>%
    select(cik, rquarter, cusip, value) %>%
    inner_join(
      top_performers_tbl %>%
        select(horizon_years, start_date, cik),
      join_by(cik, rquarter == start_date),
      copy = TRUE
    ) %>%
    collect()
}

compute_portfolio_weights <- function(holdings_data) {
  holdings_data %>%
    group_by(horizon_years, cik) %>%
    mutate(
      total_value = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      value_weight = value / total_value
    ) %>%
    select(horizon_years, cik, cusip, value_weight) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    group_by(horizon_years, cik, cusip) %>%
    summarise(
      value_weight = sum(value_weight, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

portfolio_vectors_to_matrices <- function(portfolio_vectors) {
  portfolio_vectors %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = cusip,
      values_from = value_weight,
      values_fill = 0
    ) %>%
    group_split(horizon_years) %>%
    set_names(unique(portfolio_vectors$horizon_years)) %>%
    map(~ {
      mat <- .x %>%
        select(-horizon_years, -cik) %>%
        as.matrix()
      rownames(mat) <- .x$cik
      mat
    })
}

cosine_similarity_from_matrix <- function(M) {
  # M is a matrix where each ROW is one fund's portfolio (weights across CUSIPs)
  # Returns: symmetric matrix of cosine similarities between all fund pairs

  # Compute L2 norm for each row
  row_norms <- sqrt(rowSums(M * M))

  # Normalize rows to unit length
  M_norm <- M / row_norms

  # Pairwise dot products = cosine similarities
  M_norm %*% t(M_norm)
}

extract_unique_pairs <- function(sim_matrix) {
  # Convert similarity matrix to tibble and remove self-pairs and duplicates
  as_tibble(as.table(sim_matrix), .name_repair = "minimal") %>%
    set_names(c("fund_1", "fund_2", "cos_sim")) %>%
    filter(fund_1 != fund_2) %>%
    mutate(
      pair_key = if_else(
        fund_1 < fund_2,
        paste(fund_1, fund_2, sep = "_"),
        paste(fund_2, fund_1, sep = "_")
      )
    ) %>%
    group_by(pair_key) %>%
    summarise(
      fund_1 = first(fund_1),
      fund_2 = first(fund_2),
      cos_sim = mean(cos_sim),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

get_extreme_pairs <- function(sim_matrix, horizon_label) {
  # Extract most similar and least similar fund pairs for a given horizon
  unique_pairs <- extract_unique_pairs(sim_matrix)

  most_similar <- unique_pairs %>%
    arrange(desc(cos_sim)) %>%
    slice_head(n = 1)

  least_similar <- unique_pairs %>%
    arrange(cos_sim) %>%
    slice_head(n = 1)

  bind_rows(
    most_similar %>% mutate(horizon = horizon_label, type = "most"),
    least_similar %>% mutate(horizon = horizon_label, type = "least")
  ) %>%
    select(fund_1, fund_2, cos_sim, horizon, type)
}
```

## 3.3. Backtest and Performance

```{r}
get_pair_holdings_over_time <- function(holdings_data, pairs_table) {
  # Expand pairs into individual funds and get all their holdings post-start_date
  pairs_table %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(fund_1, fund_2),
      names_to = NULL,
      values_to = "fund"
    ) %>%
    select(fund, everything()) %>%
    left_join(
      holdings_data %>%
        to_duckdb() %>%
        select(cik, rquarter, cusip, value, shares),
      join_by(fund == cik),
      relationship = "many-to-many",
      copy = TRUE
    ) %>%
    filter(rquarter >= start_date) %>%
    collect()
}

calculate_backtest_returns <- function(backtest_holdings) {
  # Aggregate holdings by horizon/type/quarter and compute quarterly returns
  backtest_holdings %>%
    group_by(horizon, type, rquarter) %>%
    summarise(
      total_value = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_holdings = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(horizon, type, rquarter) %>%
    group_by(horizon, type) %>%
    mutate(
      prev_value = lag(total_value),
      quarterly_return = (total_value - prev_value) / prev_value
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
}

compute_backtest_statistics <- function(backtest_returns) {
  # Compute performance metrics for each horizon/type combination
  backtest_returns %>%
    filter(!is.na(quarterly_return)) %>%
    group_by(horizon, type) %>%
    summarise(
      n_period = n(),
      mean_return = mean(quarterly_return),
      sd_return = sd(quarterly_return),
      sharpe_ratio = if_else(sd_return == 0, NA_real_, mean_return / sd_return),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(horizon, desc(sharpe_ratio))
}
```

# 4. Orchestration

## 4.1. Manager Selection

```{r}
# Calculate quarterly returns for all funds
returns_by_fund <- calculate_fund_returns(holdext_clean)

# Define time horizons and identify top performers
latest_rquarter <- max(returns_by_fund$rquarter)
n_top <- 10

time_horizons <- tribble(
  ~horizon_years,
  2,
  3,
  4,
  5,
) %>%
  mutate(
    quarters_back = horizon_years * 4,
    start_date = latest_rquarter - months(quarters_back * 3)
  )

top_performers <- time_horizons %>%
  mutate(
    top_funds = map(
      quarters_back,
      ~ get_top_performers(returns_by_fund, .x, latest_rquarter, n_top)
    )
  ) %>%
  unnest(top_funds) %>%
  select(-quarters_back)
```

## 4.2. Portfolio Similarity

```{r}
# Get holdings at start of each time horizon
holdings_at_start <- get_holdings_at_period_start(holdext_clean, top_performers)

# Compute portfolio weights
portfolio_vectors <- compute_portfolio_weights(holdings_at_start)

# Convert to matrices (one per horizon)
portfolio_matrices <- portfolio_vectors_to_matrices(portfolio_vectors)

# Compute cosine similarities
cosine_sims <- portfolio_matrices %>%
  map(cosine_similarity_from_matrix)

# Extract extreme pairs
extreme_pairs <- map2_dfr(
  cosine_sims,
  time_horizons$horizon_years,
  ~ get_extreme_pairs(.x, .y)
)
```

## 4.3. Backtest Performance

```{r}
# Add time horizon metadata to pairs
pair_performance_data <- extreme_pairs %>%
  left_join(
    time_horizons %>%
      select(-quarters_back),
    join_by(horizon == horizon_years)
  )

# Get holdings for paired funds over time
backtest_holdings <- get_pair_holdings_over_time(holdext_clean, pair_performance_data)

# Calculate returns
backtest_returns <- calculate_backtest_returns(backtest_holdings)

# Compute summary statistics
backtest_stats <- compute_backtest_statistics(backtest_returns)
backtest_stats
```

# 5. Results & Interpretation

Among top performers, similarity doesn't appear to be a strong predictor of risk-adjusted returns.
